markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: GTA VI’s confirmed November 19, 2026 console release creates a fixed five-month window shaping trader sentiment on Polymarket’s “What will happen before GTA VI?” market. Multiple delays—from the original Fall 2025 target to May 2026, then the current November date—have anchored expectations, with Take-Two Interactive reaffirming the timeline in recent earnings and ruling out further slips. Imminent summer marketing, including a likely third trailer and pre-order launch around late June, adds near-term visibility. Traders price roughly even odds (51–54%) on music drops such as new Playboi Carti or Rihanna albums, GPT-6’s release, or political shifts like a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire, reflecting the crowded calendar of entertainment, tech, and global events before the game’s launch.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrials in Harvey Weinstein’s New York retrials, including the May 15, 2026 deadlock on the Jessica Mann rape charge, have sharply lifted trader consensus around “No Prison Time” at 82.5%. With only one 2025 criminal-sex-act conviction standing and prosecutors weighing a potential third attempt after a June 24 hearing, the unresolved counts face mounting procedural and evidentiary hurdles. Weinstein’s separate 16-year California sentence runs consecutively but remains under appeal, while his age (73), documented health decline, and harsh conditions at Rikers have fueled expectations that additional meaningful incarceration is unlikely. These developments, combined with the high bar for further convictions in a case already tried multiple times, underpin the market’s strong lean toward minimal or no incremental prison time.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes hold an 79.5% implied probability in the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion market, driven by their 3-2 series lead over the Vegas Golden Knights following a 4-2 home victory in Game 5 on June 11. The Hurricanes have secured three wins in the best-of-seven Final, including strong performances at Lenovo Center where they have capitalized on power-play opportunities and maintained defensive structure. Vegas captured Games 1 and 3 in overtime thrillers but trails heading into Game 6 on the road. Trader consensus reflects Carolina's one-win-away positioning and recent momentum, with the Golden Knights retaining realistic comeback potential only if they can force a decisive Game 7.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks hold an 81.6% implied probability in the 2026 NBA Championship market primarily because they lead the San Antonio Spurs 3-1 in the Finals following a record 29-point comeback victory in Game 4 at Madison Square Garden. Jalen Brunson’s 36-point effort, combined with OG Anunoby’s game-winning tip-in and strong contributions from Karl-Anthony Towns and Mikal Bridges, has shifted momentum decisively toward New York. The Knicks enter Game 5 on the road one victory from their first title since 1973, while the Spurs (62-20 in the regular season) must overcome a 3-1 deficit for the first time in franchise history behind Victor Wembanyama. Traders have priced the Spurs at just 19% as a result, reflecting the steep historical odds of rallying from this position against a Knicks roster that has dominated the Eastern Conference playoffs.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France lead the 2026 World Cup winner market at implied probabilities of 16.8% and 16.1% respectively, reflecting trader consensus on their elite squads and recent form. The bunched top tier stems from a deep pool of experienced contenders, balanced group-stage draws, and minimal major injury disruptions reported in official lineups, leaving limited separation until knockout results clarify paths.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping’s continued consolidation of authority within the Chinese Communist Party and the People’s Liberation Army underpins the strong trader consensus that he will remain in power through 2026. Recent military purges and elite reshuffles have removed potential rivals without disrupting top-level stability, while Xi’s active diplomatic schedule—including state visits and engagement with foreign leaders—signals no imminent transition. The political calendar centers on the 21st Party Congress in 2027, where analysts widely expect renewal of his posts as general secretary, president, and Central Military Commission chairman, consistent with the absence of an anointed successor and the precedent of his extended third term. Speculation about health or internal challenges has surfaced periodically but lacks corroboration in official actions or verifiable elite dynamics. This environment leaves few near-term catalysts capable of altering the implied probability that Xi stays in office before 2027.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 23% in the wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market due to his high national visibility as California governor, consistent positioning as a leading critic of the current administration, and recent populist moves on AI regulation and consumer protections. No major candidates have formally declared, leaving the field fragmented among governors, senators, and 2024 figures with lower name recognition. Midterm outcomes in November 2026, early polling trends favoring higher-profile contenders, and potential consolidation around electability or fundraising could shift support, while structural factors like primary timing and voter coalitions remain fluid.
